server.BYMAX_AUTH_OPTIONS: Internal module-options injection token; consumer apps read config via ConfigService, not this token directly — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/1
server.ROLES_KEY: Internal metadata key used by RolesGuard; consumer code uses the @Roles() decorator, not the raw reflection key — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/1
server.PLATFORM_ROLES_KEY: Internal metadata key used by PlatformRolesGuard; consumer code uses @PlatformRoles(), not the raw key — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/1
server.AuthenticatedRequest: Consumer apps use @CurrentUser() user: DashboardJwtPayload instead of extending the Express Request type directly — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/2
server.PlatformAuthenticatedRequest: Consumer apps use @CurrentUser() with platform payload; raw request extension not needed — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/2
server.OAuthProviderPlugin: Extension point for custom OAuth providers; this app uses the library's built-in Google plugin — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/2
server.ResolvedOptions: Internal resolved-options type; only relevant when building library plugins, not consumer apps — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/2
nextjs.AuthProxyInstance: Return type of createAuthProxy; destructured as const { proxy } = createAuthProxy(...) so the type is covered by inference — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.ResolvedAuthProxyConfig: Internal resolved proxy config; not exposed to or required by consumer apps — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.HeadersLike: Structural interface used for mocking in library tests; not needed in application code — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.RequestWithHeaders: Structural interface for mocking; not needed in application code — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.RequestWithUrl: Structural interface for mocking; not needed in application code — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.JwtHeader: Internal JWT header shape used by decodeJwtToken; rarely referenced directly by consumers — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.DecodedToken: Internal decoded-token type from decodeJwtToken; covered by inference from the return type — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.ClientRefreshHandler: Return-type alias for createClientRefreshHandler; covered by inference (const POST = createClientRefreshHandler(...)) — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.ClientRefreshHandlerConfig: Config type for createClientRefreshHandler; covered by inline object inference — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.LogoutHandler: Return-type alias for createLogoutHandler; covered by inference — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.LogoutHandlerConfig: Union config type for createLogoutHandler; covered by inline object inference — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.LogoutHandlerRedirectConfig: Discriminated union member of LogoutHandlerConfig; not referenced directly — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.LogoutHandlerStatusConfig: Discriminated union member of LogoutHandlerConfig; not referenced directly — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.SilentRefreshHandler: Return-type alias for createSilentRefreshHandler; covered by inference — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.SilentRefreshHandlerConfig: Config type for createSilentRefreshHandler; covered by inline object inference — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.ParsedSetCookie: Internal cookie-parsing result type; used only inside the proxy — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.dedupeSetCookieHeaders: Low-level Set-Cookie deduplication utility used internally by the proxy; consumer apps do not process cookie headers directly — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.getSetCookieHeaders: Low-level utility for extracting Set-Cookie headers; used internally by the proxy — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
nextjs.parseSetCookieHeader: Low-level Set-Cookie header parser; used internally by the proxy — see https://github.com/bymaxone/nest-auth-example/issues/3
